name: Order of the Solar Temple
description: The Order of the Solar Temple (Ordre du Temple Solaire or OTS) was a secret society that purported to be a modern revival of the Knights Templar, combining elements of Rosicrucianism, New Age spirituality, and UFO conspiracy theories. Founded in 1984 by Joseph Di Mambro and Luc Jouret, the OTS preached a doctrine of spiritual purification and preparation for an impending transition to a higher plane of existence, often associated with the star Sirius. Proponents believed Earth was on the verge of ecological collapse and a new world order would emerge. They amassed a following primarily in Switzerland, France, and Canada. The group gained notoriety in the mid-1990s following a series of mass suicides and murders in Switzerland and Canada, orchestrated by the leaders and involving ritualistic elements. These events led to widespread condemnation and the group's effective dissolution. Mainstream perspectives view the OTS as a dangerous cult led by charismatic figures who manipulated vulnerable individuals, exploiting their fears and anxieties for personal gain and control. Scholarly analysis focuses on the sociological and psychological factors that contributed to the group's appeal and the tragic consequences of its beliefs, often highlighting the dangers of charismatic leadership and the potential for extremism within new religious movements. The suicides are generally attributed to a combination of apocalyptic beliefs, financial pressures, and power struggles within the organization.
subtitle: Doomsday cult with Templar pretenses.

claims.claim: The Earth is on the verge of ecological collapse, requiring spiritual purification.
claims.evidence: Di Mambro and Jouret's pronouncements, selectively interpreted environmental data.
claims.criticism: Exaggerated and unsubstantiated claims used to instill fear and control.

claims.claim: Members would ascend to a higher plane of existence on a planet orbiting Sirius.
claims.evidence: Spiritual revelations and channeled messages from ascended masters.
claims.criticism: Pseudoscientific and lacking any empirical basis. Exploitation of belief in the paranormal.

claims.claim: The OTS was a continuation of the original Knights Templar's esoteric teachings.
claims.evidence: Symbolism and rituals borrowed from Templar lore and Rosicrucianism.
claims.criticism: Historical inaccuracies and misinterpretations of Templar history. A superficial appropriation of historical imagery for legitimacy.

keyFigures.name: Jean-François Mayer
keyFigures.role: Sociologist studying new religious movements, critic of the OTS

timeline.event: Order of the Solar Temple founded by Di Mambro and Jouret
